response: convert the art collection tax by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in a 19% effective rate on the $2 million valuation which yields $380,000. convert the real estate tax calculation for the initial $5 million value by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2 million, totaling $850,000. convert the securities portfolio tax by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value and applying the 15% rate to the remaining $2.9 million, which equals $435,000. convert the initial total estate tax liability by summing these three figures to reach a combined amount of $1,665,000. convert the real estate valuation to reflect the 10% underestimation correction, raising the value from $5 million to $5.5 million. convert the new real estate tax by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2.5 million, resulting in a new liability of $950,000. convert the final total tax liability by adding the unchanged art and securities taxes to the corrected real estate tax, yielding a new total of $1,765,000.
